heading: Prohibition on provision of certain assistance
label: 10011 Prohibition on provision of certain assistance
text: No recipient of funds under this chapter shall use such funds to provide financial assistance to students to attend private elementary or secondary schools, unless such funds are used to provide special education and related services to children with disabilities, as authorized by the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1400
notes: References in TextThe Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, referred to in text, is title VI of , , , which is classified generally to chapter 33 (§ 1400 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.Pub. L. 91–23084 Stat. 175section 1400 of this titleApr. 13, 1970

Amendments2009— inserted before period at end “, unless such funds are used to provide special education and related services to children with disabilities, as authorized by the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.)”.Pub. L. 111–820 U.S.C. 1400
